free_text: Examination of break-up fusion in the 16O + 148Nd
system through measurements of forward recoil range
distributions and angular distributions
Central University of Jharkhand, Ranchi
15UD Pelletron accelerator
For forward recoil range distributions the target of
148Nd were prepared vacuum evaporation technique.
The target material was deposited on an Al backing of
thickness ~ 40-60 mg/cm2.
For angular distributions the target of 148Nd having
thickness ~ 0.30 mg/cm2 supported by Al backing of
thickness ~ 0.5 mm.
High-purity germanium gamma-ray spectroscopy
coupled to a PC through CAMAC calibrated using standard
152Eu gamma-source.
The energy resolutions of detector was ~ 2 keV for
1.408 MeV gamma-ray of 152Eu source.
For forward recoil range
distributions the stack was irradiated about 11 hrs.
For angular distributions measurement the stack was
irradiated for ~ 9 hrs.
The beam current was ~ 2-3 pnA during the irradiation
of targets.
The overall uncertainty including
statistical errors (<15%)
Data received from Pankaj K. Giri
VT
